title: Public encouraged to ‘cool off in culture’ as second heatwave of the year hits and health alerts issued
content: The BFI, Arts Council England, LIVE Green, and Julie’s Bicycle have partnered to roll out Cool Off in Culture, a UK-wide campaign signposting the public to cultural venues during increasingly frequent heatwaves.
snippet: The BFI, Arts Council England, LIVE Green, and Julie’s Bicycle have partnered to roll out Cool Off in Culture, a UK-wide campaign signposting the public to cultural venues during increasingly frequent

title: British Film Institute (BFI)
content: The British Film Institute is a film and television charitable organisation which promotes and preserves independent and mainstream filmmaking and television in the United Kingdom.
snippet: The British Film Institute is a film and television charitable organisation which promotes and preserves independent and mainstream filmmaking and television in the United Kingdom.

title: Navigating the Indian power sector in the face of extreme heat: A scoping note
content: This scoping note examines how intensifying heat-waves threaten every link of India’s power-sector value chain and outlines a roadmap for climate-ready electricity systems.
snippet: This scoping note examines how intensifying heat-waves threaten every link of India’s power-sector value chain and outlines a roadmap for climate-ready electricity systems.

title: Heat smart schools
content: Heat Smart Schools is a guidance framework by CDRI to help schools adapt to rising extreme heat risks. With over two billion children projected to face frequent heatwaves by 2050, the document emphasizes that heat threatens health, learning, and equity.
snippet: is a guidance framework by CDRI to help schools adapt to rising extreme heat risks. With over two billion children projected to face frequent heatwaves by 2050, the document

title: High-severity fires burn 30 times more acreage than 40 years ago, researchers find
content: Forest fires now burn ten times more acreage annually than in 1985, while wildfire severity has gotten even worse. In California, 30 times more acreage burned from high-severity, forest-killing fires, according to new UCLA research.
snippet: Forest fires now burn ten times more acreage annually than in 1985, while wildfire severity has gotten even worse. In California, 30 times more acreage burned from high-severity, forest-killing fires,

title: El Niño is coming. Here is where the risks to agriculture are highest
content: FAO experts map where crops and pasturelands are most vulnerable to drought.
snippet: FAO experts map where crops and pasturelands are most vulnerable to drought.

title: Understanding the global landscape of heat early warning systems
content: This report provides a global overview Heat Early Warning Systems, examining where they exist, how they are designed and operated, and their challenges and opportunities.
snippet: This report provides a global overview Heat Early Warning Systems, examining where they exist, how they are designed and operated, and their challenges and opportunities.

title: HERA launches the world’s first heat-pregnancy insurance to protect expectant mothers from extreme heat
content: HERA and its partners launched HERA Materna, the world’s first ever heat-pregnancy insurance program. Extreme heat is threatening pregnant women on the frontlines of the climate crisis, and until now no financial protection has existed to shield them.
snippet: HERA and its partners launched HERA Materna, the world’s first ever heat-pregnancy insurance program. Extreme heat is threatening pregnant women on the frontlines of the climate crisis, and until now

title: Increase in wildfire-driven ozone linked to premature deaths across the U.S.
content: Smog from wildfires is getting worse across much of the U.S., according to a NASA-funded study.
snippet: Smog from wildfires is getting worse across much of the U.S., according to a NASA-funded study.

title: Heat waves can leave homes dangerously hot – even for young, healthy adults
content: Most people know that heat waves can be dangerous. What they may not realize is that the heat indoors can be much worse than outdoors.
snippet: Most people know that heat waves can be dangerous. What they may not realize is that the heat indoors can be much worse than outdoors.

title: Chronic heat: impacts and solutions from South-East Asia and East Africa
content: While populations in continental regions are predominantly exposed to seasonal cumulative excess heat, many populations in tropical and subtropical climates are chronically exposed to high temperatures and high humidity.
snippet: While populations in continental regions are predominantly exposed to seasonal cumulative excess heat, many populations in tropical and subtropical climates are chronically exposed to high

title: World Risk Poll 2026 report: Alone together: The hidden consensus on climate change
content: This report explores public perceptions of the threat of climate change - perceptions that are shaped by people’s lived experience of risk in their daily lives as much as by scientific evidence.
snippet: This report explores public perceptions of the threat of climate change - perceptions that are shaped by people’s lived experience of risk in their daily lives as much as by scientific evidence.

title: Tracking the long journey of smoke from wildfires
content: When we see a forest fire on the news, it is easy to think of it as a local emergency. A new CMCC study shows that smoke from Italian forest fires can travel hundreds to even thousands of kilometers, carrying fine particles.
snippet: When we see a forest fire on the news, it is easy to think of it as a local emergency. A new CMCC study shows that smoke from Italian forest fires can travel hundreds to even thousands of kilometers,

title: Unpacking disaster risk reduction (DRR): Building practical approaches to identify and reduce risks
content: AIDR is offering this workshop for anyone who has a role in reducing risk, building resilience, or wants to take action in their work or community.
snippet: AIDR is offering this workshop for anyone who has a role in reducing risk, building resilience, or wants to take action in their work or community.

title: Disaster recovery for all
content: AIDR is offering this workshop for delegates seeking a deeper exploration of disaster recovery. We welcome all interested delegates, with anticipated interest from all levels of government, community organisations, agencies and researchers.

title: Statement on Venezuela earthquakes by Kamal Kishore, Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Disaster Risk Reduction
content: Statement on Venezuela earthquakes by Kamal Kishore, Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General for Disaster Risk Reduction

title: Counting the cost of heat: the case for urgent solutions for cities
content: This report reveals that extreme heat is emerging as one of the most underestimated threats to economic development worldwide, hammering city economies, overwhelming health systems, and falling hardest on women working on the frontlines of a hotter world.
snippet: This report reveals that extreme heat is emerging as one of the most underestimated threats to economic development worldwide, hammering city economies, overwhelming health systems, and falling

title: Extreme heat costing poor women workers over $57 billion in earnings a year
content: A landmark global economic analysis, paired with a state-of-the-art heat solution cost-benefit calculator covering 11,408 cities worldwide, shows that investing in heat action saves billions in losses.
snippet: A landmark global economic analysis, paired with a state-of-the-art heat solution cost-benefit calculator covering 11,408 cities worldwide, shows that investing in heat action saves billions in

title: Heat stress exposure climbed from 16% to 22% worldwide over 50 years, study shows
content: The number of people exposed to dangerous heat stress worldwide has risen sharply over the past half-century, propelled by climate change, according to a study released Monday as Europe sweltered through a punishing heat wave.
snippet: The number of people exposed to dangerous heat stress worldwide has risen sharply over the past half-century, propelled by climate change, according to a study released Monday as Europe sweltered

title: Handbook on macroeconomic modelling for climate resilience
content: This handbook provides guidance for designing and implementing technical assistance on macroeconomic modelling to support climate resilient economic development in partner countries.
snippet: This handbook provides guidance for designing and implementing technical assistance on macroeconomic modelling to support climate resilient economic development in partner countries.

title: Preparing for El Niño: Keeping teachers and learners safe during climate disasters
content: This is a webinar on preparing education systems for natural and climate-induced hazards related to this year’s El Niño weather system.
snippet: This is a webinar on preparing education systems for natural and climate-induced hazards related to this year’s El Niño weather system.

title: Policy brief: Strengthening local government action on urban heat in Sri Lankan cities: UHI-City resilience scorecard & comprehensive guidance note
content: This study seeks to understand how UHI risks are perceived, prioritised, and addressed in Sri Lankan cities, identify barriers and enablers influencing local government action.
snippet: This study seeks to understand how UHI risks are perceived, prioritised, and addressed in Sri Lankan cities, identify barriers and enablers influencing local government action.

title: From urban heat islands to resilient cities: A conceptual framework for resilient and sustainable urban environments
content: This study aims to synthesise existing knowledge on urban heat island mechanisms, patterns, and impacts; review strategies for reducing UHI and heat risks; and develop a conceptual framework to support urban heat resilience and sustainable city planning.
snippet: This study aims to synthesise existing knowledge on urban heat island mechanisms, patterns, and impacts; review strategies for reducing UHI and heat risks; and develop a conceptual framework to

title: Harnessing NISAR: Next-generation radar observations for earth applications
content: This training introduces the NISAR data products, and applications for ecosystem monitoring and disaster response. Participants will learn data access tools, SAR analysis techniques, and how NISAR supports flood, landslide, and earthquake risk assessment
snippet: This training introduces the NISAR data products, and applications for ecosystem monitoring and disaster response. Participants will learn data access tools, SAR analysis techniques, and how NISAR

title: How hazards turn into disasters: Perspectives of emergency responders
content: This study investigates how disaster and emergency management professionals prioritize the factors that shape disaster risk and resilience across different contexts.
snippet: This study investigates how disaster and emergency management professionals prioritize the factors that shape disaster risk and resilience across different contexts.

title: Expanding the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction to include animals
content: Policy paper proposing an animal-inclusive approach to the Sendai Framework by integrating animal welfare, biodiversity and sentient beings into disaster risk reduction.
snippet: Policy paper proposing an animal-inclusive approach to the Sendai Framework by integrating animal welfare, biodiversity and sentient beings into disaster risk reduction.

title: Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants
content: Rising temperatures can affect our power supply, including nuclear and natural-gas power plants
snippet: Rising temperatures can affect our power supply, including nuclear and natural-gas power plants

title: Making Delhi Heat-Resilient: A roadmap with the focus on vulnerable groups
content: The study aims to assess Delhi's increasing heat stress and looks into propose roadmap that combines year-round city-wide heat resilience measures with targeted interventions for vulnerable groups.
snippet: The study aims to assess Delhi's increasing heat stress and looks into propose roadmap that combines year-round city-wide heat resilience measures with targeted interventions for vulnerable groups.

title: Climate change looks different to different people
content: Older people in Austria widely perceive an increase in extreme weather since their childhood. Yet how strongly they notice these changes depends on their social position, where they live and how they relate to environmental issues.

title: Karl Landsteiner University of Health Sciences (KL)
content: KL is committed to fostering new ideas and great careers: Building on its values and , the university is laying the foundation for outstanding professional and human achievements and is thus playing a key role in shaping the health sciences.

title: Enhancing women’s resilience to climate-change shocks through Self-Help Groups (SHG) initiative in Somalia
content: In Somaliland, recurrent drought and climate shocks have severely disrupted livelihoods, leaving agro-pastoral communities struggling to sustain their income and food security. The women of Burao had watched the drought take almost everything.

title: Hidden seismicity patterns before large earthquakes uncovered
content: Machine learning methods have for some time been successfully applied to unravel the complexity of earthquake interactions and to identify distinct patterns in existing earthquake data catalogues.
snippet: Machine learning methods have for some time been successfully applied to unravel the complexity of earthquake interactions and to identify distinct patterns in existing earthquake data catalogues.

title: As monsoon transforms, study warns of deadly heat-rain double threat—and reveals a 2-year prediction breakthrough
content: As India grapples with record heat and a delayed monsoon, the research paints a sobering picture: climate change is creating a deadly "dual threat" of extreme humid heat and catastrophic rainfall.
snippet: As India grapples with record heat and a delayed monsoon, the research paints a sobering picture: climate change is creating a deadly "dual threat" of extreme humid heat and catastrophic rainfall.

title: Climate warnings need to be told in tangible ways to prevent disaster
content: England is sweltering under an red heat health alert and could see its hottest June day on record. In North America, football fans and players are suffering, with a quarter of this summer’s World Cup matches forecast to be played in dangerous heat.

title: This city had a flooding problem. So it turned to an animal that had been extinct there for 400 years
content: A few hundred feet from a McDonald’s and a strip mall, sandwiched between busy and polluted roads, a lush urban wetland is being built by an unusual and furry group of city residents: beavers.
snippet: A few hundred feet from a McDonald’s and a strip mall, sandwiched between busy and polluted roads, a lush urban wetland is being built by an unusual and furry group of city residents: beavers.

title: The Eyewall
content: The site is dedicated to covering tropical activity in the Atlantic Ocean, Caribbean Sea, and Gulf of Mexico. The Eyewall was founded in June 2023 by Matt Lanza and Eric Berger, who work together on the Houston-based forecasting site Space City Weather.

title: PUB launches guidebook for building owners, developers to help flood-proof their premises
content: Install mobile flood barriers to keep flood waters out. Apply waterproof coatings to protect exteriors from water damage. Build raised entrances to increase thresholds above flood levels.
snippet: Install mobile flood barriers to keep flood waters out. Apply waterproof coatings to protect exteriors from water damage. Build raised entrances to increase thresholds above flood levels.

title: How India inspired Pakistan’s law on disaster management
content: A senior Indian bureaucrat shared a copy of India’s Disaster Management Act with a Pakistani bureaucrat. What happened next was a cut,copy, paste job which made history in Pakistan’s policy circles.
snippet: A senior Indian bureaucrat shared a copy of India’s Disaster Management Act with a Pakistani bureaucrat. What happened next was a cut,copy, paste job which made history in Pakistan’s policy circles.

title: Madras Courier
content: The Madras Courier is a 233-year-old news brand, launched on October 12, 1785, by Richard Johnston, who had earlier that year inspired The Daily Universal Register, the forerunner of The Times, London.
snippet: The Madras Courier is a 233-year-old news brand, launched on October 12, 1785, by Richard Johnston, who had earlier that year inspired The Daily Universal Register, the forerunner of The Times,

title: It’s too hot in Europe–again
content: Europeans are experiencing their second heat wave this summer. One climate scientist called the weather event a “sad inevitability.”
snippet: Europeans are experiencing their second heat wave this summer. One climate scientist called the weather event a “sad inevitability.”

title: Fishermen in the Bahamas are collecting data that could improve forecasts
content: Fishing boats equipped with sensors are gathering data on ocean depth and temperature.
snippet: Fishing boats equipped with sensors are gathering data on ocean depth and temperature.

title: Extreme heat is harming remote First Nations communities. It’s time we listen to them
content: In remote communities, many homes trap heat. This is because houses are not typically designed for local climate conditions. Frequent power shortages and a lack of repair services leave these communities even more exposed.

title: Building resilience by design: from wildfires to broader challenges
content: PreventionWeb spoke with Dr Karma, Director of the European Centre for Forest Fires (ECFF), about her career path, ECFF’s work and her perspective on growing wildfire risks and related challenges.
snippet: PreventionWeb spoke with Dr Karma, Director of the European Centre for Forest Fires (ECFF), about her career path, ECFF’s work and her perspective on growing wildfire risks and related challenges.

title: Global risk communication network
content: The Global Risk Communication Network is a worldwide community of researchers, experts, and practitioners dedicated to improving how risks are communicated before, during, and after emergencies.
snippet: The Global Risk Communication Network is a worldwide community of researchers, experts, and practitioners dedicated to improving how risks are communicated before, during, and after emergencies.
